title: issue-solution-plan
description: Persisted per-issue task DAG emitted by the Plan stage (python-architect synthesis). Structured in waves with per-task staffing, interdependencies, and checkpoints. This is the B4 PLAN MEMENTO the pipeline child reloads before every wave and every spawn.

design_brief_ref.description: Path/anchor in plan.md to the Ideate design_brief.

acceptance_shape.description: Observable conditions that define DONE for the whole issue (the B5 contract from Ideate). Verified at acceptance close before the PR opens.

id.description: Stable kebab-case id, unique within the plan.

type.description: Selects the per-task typed coverage gate (implement-<type>.md).

files_hint.description: REQUIRED. The complete set of files/globs this task is allowed to touch. The wave gate enforces this deterministically (git diff --name-only): a task that touches a file outside its files_hint, or two tasks in a wave whose files_hint overlap, is a planning error and fails the wave. Shared/generated files must be assigned to exactly ONE task in the wave.

staff.description: Persona that implements this task. Default python-architect; may be devx-ux-expert for surface/UX tasks. Name only -- the repo persona is referenced, never re-implemented.

deps.description: Ids of tasks that MUST complete (and integrate) before this task starts. Drives the topological wave assignment.

wave.description: Wave index. All tasks in one wave MUST be mutually independent (no intra-wave deps) so their branches integrate without conflict by construction.

acceptance.description: How this single task is proven (the typed coverage gate it must satisfy).

checkpoint.description: What the wave gate verifies for this task after integration.

goal.description: One line: what integrated state this wave establishes.

status.description: Resume marker. 'integrated' = fast-forwarded onto the issue branch (immutable evidence); the executor never re-runs it. 'superseded' = replaced by a re-plan. Defaults to pending.

base_sha.description: The issue-branch sha this wave was integrated on top of (set at run time). Lets a crashed pipeline reset a half-done wave to a known-clean base.

integration_check.description: Optional extra check the orchestrator runs after integrating the wave (e.g. full suite, a specific scenario).

replan_count.description: Number of re-plans-from-failed-wave consumed. Hard cap 2 (see wave-gate-rubric.md).

active_from_wave.description: Resume point. The executor starts at this wave, not wave 1; waves below it are already 'integrated' on the issue branch. A re-plan re-emits this wave and later waves and sets this to the failed wave's index.
